title: Erythrococca tristis
taxonomy: Erythrococca tristis is classified within the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the spurge family. This family comprises around 300 genera and 5,000 species of plants, many of which are known for their toxic milky sap. The Euphorbiaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Erythrococca tristis falling into the subfamily Acalyphoideae. This subfamily is characterized by its diverse range of plant species, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions. The exact classification of Erythrococca tristis may be subject to further research and revision.

intro: Erythrococca tristis, also known as the "garden rue", is a plant species within the family Euphorbiaceae, characterized by its dark green, glossy leaves and clusters of small, greenish-yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Erythrococca tristis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and diterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Erythrococca tristis is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is commonly used to treat fever, cough, and skin conditions, making it a valuable remedy in folk medicine.
